critic_raw:
This SDOH hospital readmission prediction experiment has specific methodological concerns.

First, stratified random splitting is used on five years of EHR data. Hospital readmission data has temporal structure — treatment protocols change, patient populations shift. A random split leaks temporal context. A temporal split would be more appropriate.

Second, the comparison design conflates two variables: the primary model is gradient-boosted trees on all features (clinical + SDOH), while the baseline is logistic regression on clinical features only. This confounds model architecture with feature set. To isolate SDOH feature contribution, the same architecture should be used with and without SDOH features.

Third, SDOH features (income, education, housing status) are often imputed or administratively linked. The design does not address the quality, coverage, or missingness rate of SDOH features — which may correlate with the outcome.

Fourth, AUC-ROC does not reflect the operational decision context. Clinical deployment typically involves identifying a prioritized high-risk set, better captured by precision/recall at a fixed operating point.

Fifth, stratification checks across demographic subgroups is post-hoc confound analysis, not control.
all_issues_raised: Random split on temporally structured EHR data
Architecture confounded with feature set — GBT+SDOH vs. LR-clinical conflates two variables
SDOH feature quality, coverage, and missingness unaddressed
AUC-ROC insufficient for clinical operating point decisions
Demographic subgroup analysis is post-hoc, not control

round_transcripts.critic: The primary design flaw is conflating model architecture with feature set: GBT+SDOH vs. LR-clinical cannot isolate SDOH feature value. Random split on temporally structured EHR data causes leakage. SDOH feature quality and missingness are unaddressed. AUC-ROC is insufficient for clinical deployment decisions. Demographic subgroup analysis is post-hoc.
round_transcripts.defender: On architecture confounding: this is the most important critique. The design cannot attribute improvements to SDOH features vs. GBT architecture. A 2x2 factorial (architecture × feature set) is needed. The defender concedes this is a meaningful design flaw. On temporal split: for measuring signal strength in a research context, a random split can be appropriate — deployment generalization requires temporal split. On SDOH quality: missingness patterns for SDOH features are not random and may correlate with outcomes — must be addressed. On AUC-ROC: clinical deployment decisions require operating-point evaluation. On subgroup analysis: post-hoc, conceded.

round_transcripts.critic: The defense concedes the architecture confounding as the primary flaw. The core hypothesis — that SDOH features improve prediction — cannot be cleanly tested by this design. All other issues are additionally acknowledged.
round_transcripts.defender: Fully accepting architecture confounding as a fundamental design flaw. The experiment would need a 2x2 factorial design to isolate SDOH feature contribution.

all_issues_adjudicated: Architecture confounded with feature set — fundamental flaw, NOT resolved
AUC-ROC insufficient for clinical operating point — acknowledged
